sections.alt: Evocon office dashboard — OEE, scrap, quantity and downtime charts
content: I was an in-house designer at Evocon, a startup helping manufacturers improve production performance and waste less time and material. The work meant designing for two very different audiences at once. On one side was the office software, used on both PC and mobile by managers and office staff and built around large volumes of data represented through extensive tables and graphs.

sections.alt: Evocon improvement view — reduce machine reset time
content: The goal was to make all of that genuinely easy to understand — to help people pinpoint the exact problem holding their production back, see whether they'd been improving over time, and read real-time info on how every production line was running.
Designing for two opposite users at once: mountains of factory data turned into one clear answer, and a shop-floor screen simple enough to ask almost nothing.

sections.alt: Evocon shop-floor tablet — factory view with live station status
content: On the other side was the tablet software for the operators on the production lines. Here the challenge was the opposite: keep it dead simple and ask for as few button presses as possible to get the necessary info from the shop floor up to office management. It also had to make slowdowns impossible to miss — where production was lagging and why — so the floor could act the moment it happened.

Alongside the product work, I led Evocon's rebranding. Part of Evocon's mission is to teach its users about efficient production, so the brand needed to feel more contemporary and friendlier to match.

sections.alt: Evocon brand illustration — friendly character on the factory floor
content: I shaped that through a friendly character, fun animations, and really easy-to-understand illustrations built to get the main idea across at a glance — work that stretched my range well beyond product UX into brand, illustration, and motion. In 2019, Evocon won the EAS award for Best Design Implementation.

images.alt: Evocon animated icon set — production concepts
Evocon animated icon set — production concepts

content: Simple animated icons that trigger on hover (or on press on tablets) that simplify common concepts used in Evocon.
